examples.title: A Car Driving Down a Road
examples.content: A car starts at a street sign (position 0). After 10 seconds, it's 100 meters past the sign. The car changed position. That's motion. The car is moving relative to the road.

examples.title: A Ball Thrown in the Air
examples.content: You throw a ball straight up. It goes up (motion), reaches a peak (still motion—changing height), comes back down (motion). The whole time, position changes. That's motion.

examples.title: You Sitting on a Moving Train
examples.content: You're sitting still on a train seat (relative to the train, you're not moving). But the train is moving 100 km/h (relative to the ground, you ARE moving). Motion is relative!
